question_text: A car tyre has a textured, bumpy surface while an ice skate blade is smooth and polished. Explain why the textured tyre surface provides better grip than the smooth blade.
correct_answer: The textured/bumpy surface of the tyre has a larger surface area and catches on the road material, creating more friction and better grip. The smooth blade minimises contact and friction, allowing sliding on ice.
explanation: Friction depends on the roughness of surfaces and the area of contact. A textured surface increases both factors, giving better grip, while a smooth surface reduces both and allows sliding.
